texto: When we were little kids, 'friend' wasn't a verb. You didn't 'friend' someone. You had friends. It was only a noun. It didn't multitask. It was a simpler time, Hen.
explicacao: Citação de Daniel Ehrenhaft sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “When we were little kids, 'friend' wasn't a verb. You didn't 'friend' someone. You had friends. I…” articula reflexao, ya, friend em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e reflexao.

frase_original: When we were little kids, 'friend' wasn't a verb. You didn't 'friend' someone. You had friends. It was only a noun. It didn't multitask. It was a simpler time, Hen.

texto: There she stood. Dark circles ringed her eyes. Her face was pale, almost snow-white. She probably hadn't slept, either. She was still wearing the same dress. Her hair looked like a bomb had gone off. She was beautiful.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Daniel Ehrenhaft: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “There she stood. Dark circles ringed her eyes. Her face was pale, almost snow-white. She probably…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, ya, there pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e reflexao.

frase_original: There she stood. Dark circles ringed her eyes. Her face was pale, almost snow-white. She probably hadn't slept, either. She was still wearing the same dress. Her hair looked like a bomb had gone off. She was beautiful.
